Process Roadmap

How the validation program works — and where AI accelerated the build
Timesheet Validation · Built with AI

A data-quality & compliance framework, accelerated by AI.

Detecting miss-plotting of work hours and misuse of paid time across the employee timesheet journey. Domain expertise designed the logic; an AI assistant compressed the build at each stage — from data wrangling to a board-ready dashboard.

AT
Architected by Archival Talanay
Process stage AI-assisted acceleration
1

Data Sources

Three systems feed the program — workforce management (schedules, attendance), the timesheet portal (plotted hours), and the HR roster (employee attributes, employment status).
✦ AIAI mapped and reconciled the source fields and flagged join keys, removing days of manual data dictionary work.
2

Consolidation

All sources merge into one unified fact table — a single row per employee per day — forming the backbone for every downstream check and report.
✦ AIAI built the full outer join and the star schema from a plain-language brief, turning intent into a working data model in minutes.
3

Scrub & Validation

Four checks run on every row — date accuracy, plotting validity, plotting category, and submission timeliness — each written back as a clear status.
✦ AIAI debugged the parsing logic and repaired the balancing rules when hours came back blank — resolved in minutes, not days.
4

Miss-plotting Detection

Catches three patterns: dates plotted to the wrong day, charge codes that don’t match attendance, and offset entries that conceal invalid plotting.
✦ AIAI encoded the detection rules and edge cases — including per-label offset balancing — directly from worked examples.
5

Paid-hours Integrity

Reviews paid-leave misuse, adjustment-recovery misuse, and unsupported tardy or unpaid deductions to protect the integrity of paid time.
✦ AIAI reconciled recovered vs. eligible hours and surfaced misuse logic that would otherwise need painstaking spreadsheet auditing.
6

Validation Flags

Each row carries computed flags — valid, invalid, recovery, accuracy, and category — the single source of truth consumed by all reporting.
✦ AIAI generated and verified the flag logic, then sanity-checked totals against the source so the numbers reconcile.
7

Compliance Reporting

An interactive, self-contained dashboard with agent drill-downs, supervisor and OM roll-ups, and filtered downloads — plus a structured exception report for corrective action.
✦ AIAI authored the entire dashboard — KPIs, charts, filters, the attendance calendar, and a financial simulator — and iterated on request without rebuilding.
8

Analytics Layer

A star-schema BI model for executive and management reporting — trends, validity and timeliness rates, recovery percentages, and headcount views.
✦ AIAI produced the model, the documentation, and reusable prompts, making the whole pipeline repeatable and easy to hand over.
Faster Delivery

Weeks of build effort compressed into days through AI assistance.

Higher Accuracy

Consistent rules and reconciled totals reduce manual error.

Clear Visibility

One dashboard gives leaders compliance insight at a glance.

Repeatable Process

Documented logic and prompts make every refresh reproducible.

The scrub logic, validation rules, dashboard, and analytics model were designed by Archival Talanay and built with AI assistance. · Last updated June 2026
